danger_signals.visual_form: fast attack animations and combo sequences

danger_signals.meaning: player vulnerability and imminent defeat risk

danger_signals.visual_form: larger character sprites with distinct visual styling

safety_signals.visual_form: brief animation pause after successful attack lands
safety_signals.meaning: temporary protection window during combo opportunities

safety_signals.visual_form: blocking stance animation with reduced damage feedback

faction_indicators.faction: Fatal Fury protagonist group (Andy Bogard, Mai Shiranui, Terry Bogard lineage)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Boss characters (Billy Kane, Axel Hawk, Laurence Blood, Wolfgang Krauser, Geese Howard) display more threatening/intimidating character designs
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Italian stage setting shown in gameplay screenshot suggests international tournament setting

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Secret boss character with distinct visual presentation from core roster

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: sequential opponent roster expansion - starts with eight regular characters, progresses through ten opponents, culminates in five boss encounters
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: difficulty scaling through increasingly challenging boss AI opponents (Billy, Axel, Laurence, Geese, Krauser progression)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ryo Sakazaki appears as dream match opponent with special status
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: conditional appearance requiring perfect two-round victory condition across all matches

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: four previously CPU-only boss characters become playable, indicating power tier shift
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: expanded roster changes competitive viability landscape

environmental_storytelling.example: Italy stage backdrop establishes international tournament context and geographic diversity
Boss characters display more imposing/detailed sprite work compared to regular roster, communicating threat level through artistic prominence
Detailed and humorous background elements contribute to world-building atmosphere and character context

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: brief invincibility window after landing attack hit, enabling combo continuation
can perform low line jump attacks by pressing light punch/kick button when opponent is on opposite line
increased aggression and complexity in Billy, Axel, Laurence, Geese, and Krauser encounter sequences
standard combat response patterns established across eight foundational character roster

notes: Fatal Fury Special uses arcade fighting game visual conventions typical of early 1990s SNK design. World-state communication occurs primarily through tournament structure progression, opponent character selection hierarchy, and sprite-based visual design differentiation between regular and boss characters. Faction identity is implicit through character roster grouping rather than explicit visual markers like color-coding or heraldry. Environmental storytelling emphasizes international tournament setting through stage backdrops and character visual variety. The game's manifestation layer relies on combat feedback animations (health bars, hit confirmation pauses, invincibility frames) and character sprite prominence to communicate mechanical and narrative state.
